item.title: Twins Jack and Artie Porter hear their mum for the first time
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Thunderbirds first team through to Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: The Adelaide Thunderbirds are through to their third Super Netball grand final in four years. Now they wait to see if they will face the Melbourne Vixens or West Coast Fever for a shot at the title.

item.title: Shakespeare, Bowie, Einstein: Mona opens one-of-a-kind underground library Phrontisterion
item.description: From Shakespeare's First Folio to neon-lit shelves to a curvy kids' section, Mona's new library, Phrontisterion, is anything but ordinary.

item.title: This week in history quiz, June 22: Where was Ned Kelly finally captured?
item.description: Can you answer our questions about Anna Wintour, Australian tennis royalty and the doughnuts of the past? Test yourself.

item.title: Future Care Consultants referred hopeful students to agent that charged them thousands for 'fraudulent' visa help
item.description: Aspiring students looking to study abroad in Australia say they've been left blindsided and with their futures in doubt after an education consultancy promised them migration and visa advice.

item.title: Agriculture Minister confirms another case of H5 bird flu in Western Australia
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ABC More or Less quiz: British PMs edition
item.description: Some British PMs have held office across decades. Others have struggled to outlast a lettuce. It's time to test your knowledge.

item.title: Andy Burnham discusses COVID-19 pandemic in 2020
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Union claims Macquarie University illegally targeted staff for redundancy in Fair Work claim
item.description: Two senior academics from Macquarie University allege they were targeted for redundancy because of their union activity.

item.title: Greyhound Racing SA hands four-year ban to 'remorseful' trainer Jack Clayton Trengove
item.description: One of SA greyhound racing's most prominent identities has been disqualified after a 12-month inquiry by the sport's controlling body.

item.title: Siri Hustvedt reflects on the death of her husband, Paul Auster, in her new memoir, Ghost Stories
item.description: The author of What I Loved has published a moving memoir drawing on letters and journals documenting the last days of Paul Auster, her partner in life and writing.

item.title: Food trades, hospitality and agriculture hardest hit in vocational training drop
item.description: The number of West Australians enrolled in apprenticeships and traineeships is the lowest in five years after a post-COVID spike in numbers.

item.title: Bosnian fans lined up outside World Cup match
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Military-grade thermal imaging and helicopters were deployed to achieve the complete eradication of feral pigs from Australia's third-largest island.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Resident rescued after falling into deep hole while helping kangaroo on Raymond Island
item.description: A wildlife rescue on Raymond Island took a dramatic turn when one of the rescuers found himself trapped underground and needing urgent help.

item.title: Will bird flu in Australia affect the price of eggs?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: University graduate satisfaction with degrees at record lows, according to government-funded survey
item.description: Australian students are the least satisfied they have ever been with their university degrees, according to a long-running survey, leaving some questioning the value of their qualifications.

item.title: Double brick dominates Perth homes. Experts say it could be slowing down construction
item.description: It takes 12 to 18 months on average to build a home in Western Australia — but with non-brick frame construction, the home building timeline could be cut by up to half.

item.title: Vixens reach third straight Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: Reigning champions the Melbourne Vixens will get a chance to defend their 2025 Super Netball title after beating the West Coast Fever 62-51in the preliminary final.

item.title: Guy Montgomery is back with your weekly spelling bee fix
item.description: Feel free to exhale that breath you've been holding in — it's Sunday and that means it's time to get spelling again.

item.title: Rubbish piled up after Bali Buduk locals illegally dump trash
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The surprising reasons some bosses oppose work from home
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Ben Stokes retirement highlights his main character energy was not always a force for good
item.description: Ben Stokes has been the centre of England's cricketing universe for 15 years. In announcing his retirement mid-match, he highlighted how damaging that main character energy can be.

item.title: Artist's impression of NASA's Swift Boost Mission
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Lodging too early and other mistakes people make when doing their taxes
item.description: With side hustles, working from home, crypto investments, renting out rooms and receiving income through digital platforms or apps, experts say people are at greater risk of making errors with their tax.

item.title: Retiring Ben Stokes backs Harry Brook as England's next Test captain
item.description: Ben Stokes says vice-captain Harry Brook has his "100 per cent" support to lead England's Test side, but coach Brendon McCullum is less effusive.

item.title: Multiple vehicles destroyed after trucks crash into rest stop on Hume Highway near Goulburn
item.description: Police say it is a "miracle" no-one was seriously harmed after six semi-trailers were destroyed by fire after trucks crashed into a rest area south-west of Goulburn.

item.title: SA coroner finds 'no genuine opportunity' to prevent double-fatal Urrbrae crash
item.description: A South Australian coroner says it is an "unfortunate truth" that there was no way for police to predict or prevent a collision that killed a senior police officer and another woman in 2020.

item.title: Menswear brand accused of using AI to 'whitewash' model’s image
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australians are being scammed and used by criminal networks as money mules
item.description: Graham Guy was the victim of a romance scam and had his bank accounts drained over several years. Then came an unusual request that unwittingly drew him into crime.

item.title: July 1 brings new rules, a tax cut and an increase to paid parental leave
item.description: A bunch of new rules come into effect today. Find out how they could impact your finances.

item.title: Travel quiz, July 1: Do you have what it takes today?
item.description: Where would you find the iconic Forrest Gump bench? And do you know where this bridge is?

item.title: Real-world incidents targeting Jews reinforce 'spiral of hate' online, royal commission hears
item.description: The royal commission has heard new research which found anti-Jewish hate increased online both after October 7, 2023 and the Bondi Beach terror attack in December.

item.title: SA Police film electric motorcycle rider doing wheelie while attending another crash
item.description: SA Police publishes a video of a teenager on an electric motorcycle doing a wheelie and then crashing into a car while they were attending another collision at the same location in Adelaide's southern suburbs.

item.title: Boxing Australia CEO Dinah Glykidis resigns ahead of Commonwealth Games
item.description: Boxing Australia's CEO Dinah Glykidis has resigned, following the national integrity manager, just weeks before the Commonwealth Games.

item.title: Canada will join the Eurovision Song Contest semi-finals in Bulgaria
item.description: While Canadian Céline Dion gave Switzerland its second Eurovision victory in 1988, next year will be the first time Canada competes in the song contest.

item.title: Queensland's first koala egg and sperm bank created to preserve genetic diversity
item.description: Scientists hope the biobank will help reduce chlamydia infections in future koala populations, and act as a safety net for the survival of the endangered Australian icon.

item.title: Extremists using 'codes', emojis and more to evade social media detection, royal commission hears
item.description: Far-right individuals adopt memes as a way to push "more palatable" antisemitism, which is less likely to be picked up by social media moderators.

item.title: Flooding damages nesting sites for endangered white-throated snapping turtles
item.description: An environmental group says steep banks, loose sand and low water levels are resulting in extremely low clutch numbers.

item.title: Financial misconduct compensation scheme faces $170 million cost blowout
item.description: The First Guardian and Shield super disasters, and Dixon Advisory fallout, is causing a cost blowout in the taxpayer-funded scheme that is designed to compensate victims of financial misconduct.

item.title: Jewish human rights lawyer says she was abused for Palestinian advocacy and Israeli government criticism
item.description: The Jewish Council of Australia's executive director has been labelled a "Hitler's Jew" in what she describes to a royal commission as an online harassment campaign by pro-Israel and neo-Nazi groups.

item.title: Man dies after car hits fallen tree following severe storms near Cowra
item.description: Wind gusts of about 100 kilometres an hour were recorded as severe storms hit the NSW Central West overnight with tragic consequences.

item.title: Firmus CEOs talk about energy use for planned AI factories
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wallabies fullback Jock Campbell earns Test recall to face Ireland in Nations Championship opener
item.description: Jock Campbell emerges from almost four years in the international rugby wilderness to snare the prized fullback role for the Wallabies in their Nations Championship opener against Ireland.

item.title: Adelaide Thunderbirds fans will be in full cry at Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: Among the devoted Thunderbirds fans making the journey to Melbourne for the grand final against the Vixens are the Rawlins family, including sisters Tess and Eve.

item.title: eSafety commissioner tells royal commission Jewish community let down by cyber abuse scheme
item.description: eSafety Commissioner Julie Inman Grant became emotional while apologising to Jewish victims of social media harassment who do not meet the regulator's legal threshold for intervention.

item.title: How to keep your home warm if you're renting or on a budget
item.description: If you're searching for ways to make your home warmer, these easy and inexpensive tricks can make a big difference.

item.title: Moment Egypt clinched a World Cup win from the Socceroos
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian influencer Lily Jay's tangled web of AI manipulation
item.description: Receiving millions of views online, the Lily Jay Foundation claims to help children across the world. She was even given an award for her work. But what is real and what is AI-generated? ABC NEWS Verify investigates.

item.title: Taylor Swift's 'royal wedding' was a lesson in media mastery
item.description: After mounting speculation, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have officially tied the knot. One expert says what we know, and don't know, about the wedding is a lesson in "media mastery".

item.title: Female apprentices join Sydney Trains program, as more workers needed amid local labour target
item.description: Nicola Huggins and Ariana Gackowski are learning the wiring, welding and hand tool skills required to one day repair the trains Sydneysiders travel on every day.

item.title: Maintenance issues, outages plague WA standalone power systems
item.description: Farmers in WA's south say the standalone power systems they got from Western Power after being moved off the grid have been problematic.

item.title: International experts say surveilling local birds, marine mammals a 'priority' in bird flu response
item.description: While the H5N1 bird flu strain is relatively new to Australia, experts who have monitored the spread in other continents say surveillance is key to tackling the deadly virus.

item.title: Australia defeats England to win ICC Women's T20 Cricket World Cup
item.description: Australia has won the ICC Women's T20 World Cup in commanding style, defeating England by seven wickets in the final at Lord's.

item.title: Mount Isa barber leaves behind fresh fades and more on Mornington Island
item.description: Mount Isa barber Jon James travels to remote Mornington Island to teach barbering skills, hoping to leave behind a trade that will last long after he returns home.

item.title: Sophie Molineux praised after leading Australia to Women's T20 World Cup win
item.description: Sophie Molineux is praised for her leadership ability after guiding Australia to its Women's T20 World Cup victory.

item.title: Hobart Deputy Lord Mayor Zelinda Sherlock under fire over taking office snacks
item.description: Despite her apology and the integrity commission dismissing the case, Hobart's deputy mayor is still facing backlash after she took snacks from council premises for her home office.

item.title: Social media giants front royal commission into antisemitism
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: 'Bad blood' between US and European allies as NATO summit begins
item.description: Dozens of world leaders are descending on Türkiye this week to rubber-stamp significant NATO commitments, but the dynamics on the sidelines could prove more interesting to watch.

item.title: Pacific leaders criticise Chinese nuclear-capable missile test as 'reckless'
item.description: Solomon Islands PM says a ballistic missile test carried out by China is "not the act of a friend", sentiments echoed by several Pacific leaders.

item.title: Democrats' Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner accused of sexual assault
item.description: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner says the allegation is "categorically false", but is considering his next steps as the report prompts several key backers to withdraw their endorsements.

item.title: AFL legend Tony Modra leaves hospital after truck crash near Victor Harbor
item.description: Former Adelaide Crows and Fremantle player Tony Modra says he has left hospital after he was seriously injured in a truck crash last month.

item.title: Piggery owners face court on animal cruelty charges
item.description: Five co-owners of an Adelaide Plains piggery are collectively accused of ill-treating their animals, including allegedly failing to ensure the animals had "adequate and appropriate living conditions".

item.title: Deadly podcasts to listen to this NAIDOC Week, from Indigenuity to Inside the Huddle
item.description: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

item.title: For The People brand agency workers in battle to be paid amid liquidator investigations
item.description: Staff of an award-winning branding agency say they arrived at work one day to locked doors and a notice saying their employer was $25,000 behind on rent, and it went downhill from there.

item.title: Oral care giant Hismile used 'misleading' videos in social media advertisements
item.description: Oral care giant Hismile has used "misleading" videos in social media advertisements showing a sales professional, and its own staff, spruiking products while dressed as health workers.

item.title: CFA senior leaders accidentally send 'draft' response to fatal bushfire inquiry
item.description: An administrative error during a parliamentary inquiry has led the CFA to inadvertently reveal exactly how short-staffed its stations were during last summer's deadly fire season.

item.title: Bondi Beach terror attack survivor critical of YouTube's decision to allow 'false flag' video
item.description: Arsen Ostrovsky has criticised a decision by YouTube to keep a video on its platform, in which creators suggested he had lied about his injuries from the Bondi Beach terror attack.

item.title: Australia issued blunt warning over AUKUS and China's nuclear capabilities
item.description: Victor Gao, the vice-president of a major Chinese think tank, conducts some sabre-rattling in the wake of China's missile test in the Pacific.

item.title: Trump says US-Iran ceasefire is 'over'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Aboriginal activist Ruth Langford tells Tasmanian court of obligation to prevent 'slaughtered' forests
item.description: An Aboriginal activist has told a Tasmanian court she has a cultural obligation to "protect lands that are being slaughtered", after she was charged with trespassing while protesting in a logging coupe.

item.title: Researchers harvest water from the air to grow native seedlings and rehydrate the Wheatbelt
item.description: Can water be made from air in WA's Wheatbelt? Researchers are putting an invention to the test.

item.title: Democrats' Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner suspends midterm campaign after sexual assault accusation
item.description: The Democratic Senate candidate in the US state of Maine has suspended his midterm election campaign after a string of controversies, including an accusation of sexual assault.

item.title: Neil the seal 'may have moved on', Tasmanian wildlife experts say
item.description: Authorities in Tasmania say it appears Neil the seal may have left his resting spot at a Hobart beach and returned to sea.

item.title: Country musician Jeanette Wormald experiences copyright dispute and AI website woes
item.description: Country-folk musician Jeanette Wormald was shocked when she found a fake AI-generated website "tribute" to her, in place of a site she thought had lapsed. But then she found disputes over her copyright on her music.

item.title: Swimming Australia to take proactive, world-first approach to 'scary' fake news stories
item.description: In a world-first initiative, Swimming Australia is taking a proactive approach to deal with clickbait and fake news headlines about its athletes, including Olympic champion Mollie O'Callaghan, who has been targeted in the past.

item.title: Narendra Modi given rapturous welcome as he addresses thousands at community event in Melbourne
item.description: More than 20,000 people have given a rapturous welcome to Narendra Modi at Melbourne's Docklands Stadium as Anthony Albanese heaps praise on Australia's Indian diaspora.

item.title: AI, gambling and Middle East set for Labor conference debate
item.description: A draft document, seen by ABC, reveals AI, gambling and the Middle East are expected to feature heavily in Labor's upcoming national conference debate.

item.title: Trump's new Iran threats could mean all-out war but there are other options
item.description: Not only is the war not over, it is once again at a dangerous inflection point, as the US and Iran revert to military options to deal with differences over their interim peace agreement.

item.title: Cruz Hewitt through to Wimbledon juniors semifinal as Aussie duo falls short in mixed doubles final
item.description: Cruz Hewitt continues to better his famous father's Wimbledon juniors record after yet another convincing win at the All England Club.

item.title: Traditional owners say connection to country ignored under flawed legislation
item.description: Traditional owners from WA's Wheatbelt say a historic native title settlement has increased division among Noongar groups.

item.title: BBL to play first overseas match in India as Scorchers face Renegades
item.description: The historic BBL game in India, between Melbourne Renegades and Perth Scorchers, is every chance to be a one-off spectacular, with officials reluctant to commit beyond 2026.

item.title: Derryn Hinch, the 'human headline', former senator and broadcaster, dies aged 82
item.description: Hinch was known for his distinctive broadcast style, his time in the Senate, and his law-breaking crusades to expose sex offenders that earned him several stints in jail.

item.title: Lush native lawn ideas for waterwise, lower-maintenance gardens
item.description: If mowing the lawn is getting you down, it might be time to ditch your exotic buffalo, couch or kikuyu grass and switch to a native alternative.

item.title: China achieves first-ever controlled rocket recovery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: China achieves first-ever controlled recovery of orbital-class rocket
item.description: China has successfully tested an experimental rocket retrieval system, state media reported, in the hope of breaking US dominance in reusable rockets.

item.title: All-star cast set sail in Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey
item.description: Christopher Nolan and the cast of the highly anticipated The Odyssey sit down with ABC News Breakfast to talk about the upcoming film.

item.title: Curbing well-off taxpayers' access to key programs could save billions
item.description: Curbing access to the child care subsidy, parental leave pay, aged care and pensions for some of the most well-off taxpayers would free up about $21 billion a year, a new report says.

item.title: Living in the 'forgotten' community of Beagle Bay
item.description: Residents of the Indigenous community of Beagle Bay say they feel forgotten by their landlord: the Western Australian government.

item.title: Derryn Hinch's memorable media moments: from radio wars with prime ministers to Dancing with the Stars
item.description: A man of colourful contradictions, Derryn Hinch was a larger-than-life fixture on Australian radio and television over five decades who died on Friday morning at the age of 82.

item.title: Carbon monoxide poisoning suspected in fatal Mount Gambier incident
item.description: A 55-year-old has died and three other men have been taken to hospital after a case of suspected carbon monoxide poisoning at a Mount Gambier home in South Australia's south-east.

item.title: France dominates Wallabies to post historic Test win
item.description: Following a second-half blitz, France scores its fourth straight victory over the Wallabies, triumphing 42-26 in the Nations Championship Test in Brisbane.

item.title: The Invite welcomes you to one of the best films of 2026
item.description: Olivia Wilde's return to directing might take place almost entirely in a single room, but it unfolds all the relationship anxieties we prefer to keep hidden at home.

item.title: Can Australia avoid the next round of the global energy crisis?
item.description: As the conflict has dragged on, gas prices have soared and now threaten to up-end the economies of Europe and Asia.

item.title: Australia's first map of city zoning shows Melbourne streets ahead on density
item.description: Four years on from a national pledge to unlock more housing, only one capital city allows townhouses or three-storey developments on most of its residential land.

item.title: Grace Woodroofe on Heath Ledger, healing and turning abuse into art for new album
item.description: Heath Ledger launched her music career at just 17, but an emotionally abusive relationship with a fellow musician derailed it. A decade on, Grace Woodroofe is releasing the soundtrack to her survival.

item.title: Cost-conscious Canberra cyclists continue bracing against winter's chill
item.description: Every year, winter sees many Canberrans hanging up their helmets, despite the city being considered Australia's "cycling capital." This year though, cyclist numbers have remained relatively steady, even as the mercury drops.

item.title: NT government faces Federal Court challenge over laws capping compensation payouts
item.description: The NT government's controversial laws capping compensation for prisoners unlawfully assaulted, strip searched or imprisoned are being challenged in the Federal Court.

item.title: How a fireman helped make dinosaur fossils cool again
item.description: Travis Enright is four months into investigating what he calls "a 100-million-year-old cold case" involving a 10-metre-long aquatic dinosaur called Moria.

item.title: Love story: Motherhood is helping Moana Hope re-learn how to love
item.description: The former AFLW player was an adult when she realised her childhood had warped her understanding of love.

item.title: Can the pill affect your mental health? Young woman says mood swings were too much to bear
item.description: Two young women say taking the pill to manage painful periods had side effects they weren't expecting.

item.title: Jai Arrow vows to fight MND for everyone during 'World's Biggest Birthday Party'
item.description: Former NRL player Jai Arrow says he is determined to raise money and awareness for Motor Neurone Disease until a cure is found.

item.title: This week in history quiz, July 13: Test your recall on Taylor Swift, the Tour de France and the Salem witch trials
item.description: How much do you know about long-past British monarchs, the Salem witch trials and Nintendo games of the 80s? Find out with this week's quiz.

item.title: Small and achievable ways to drink less alcohol
item.description: If you’ve fallen off the Dry July wagon or you just aren’t interested in teetotalling, trying smaller steps to reducing your alcohol intake can still make a difference to your health.

item.title: With AI, we have returned to a world of two competing superpowers
item.description: When it comes to AI, Australia is a spectator at a UFC cage fight between the United States and China, except the spectators are inside the cage with the combatants.

item.title: As swift parrots kept breeding in this forest, the government realised it had a problem
item.description: It was late 2021 when one of Australia's rarest birds started to arrive in the native forests of Lonnavale. Ecologists had expected it but for a reacting forestry sector, it was "not sustainable".

item.title: Why the world's biggest AI companies want data centres in the NT
item.description: The NT's sunny skies, natural gas reserves and proximity to Asia have caught the attention of a dozen data centre developers.

item.title: Man's death 'not suspicious' after 'large fight' on APY Lands, police say
item.description: Police said the death of a 59-year-old man was not suspicious after a fight broke out on South Australia's APY Lands, but investigations into the disturbance are continuing.

item.title: US announces new strikes against Iran after attacks reported in Kuwait and Bahrain
item.description: Fresh attacks have rocked Iran and Kuwait as Tehran and Washington square off over the Strait of Hormuz, offering contradicting statements as to whether the vital waterway was open to traffic.

item.title: Queensland teen dies, 22 injured in e-mobility device crashes since safety laws brought in
item.description: Regulations introduced in Queensland less than a fortnight ago limit e-mobility devices to 12km/h on footpaths. Riders must be 16 and above or be supervised by a parent. They can also be breathalysed.

item.title: Sentencing of Molly Ticehurst's killer Daniel Billings brought forward
item.description: The sentencing of the man who murdered New South Wales woman Molly Ticehurst has been brought forward to allow a psychiatrist to give evidence.

item.title: Bazball is over as Brendon McCullum's deliberately uncomplicated regime falls foul of itself, leaving complicated legacy
item.description: Brendon McCullum deliberately set out to make England's men's Test team a less complicated team to play cricket for. After four years of Bazball's highs and lows, it leaves a very complicated legacy.

item.title: Thomas Tuchel's exchange with Jude Bellingham gives England another chance to prove its World Cup mettle
item.description: This England World Cup squad is proving they are different to their predecessors at every stop. Now their manager has just given them their latest challenge.

item.title: Payne Haas to stay with Samoa and says nation is 'big chance' to win Rugby League World Cup
item.description: Payne Haas has cleared the air about his World Cup plans and says there is a "great chance" Samoa can lift the trophy for the first time.

item.title: Five of our best chicken dinner recipes
item.description: From pot pie to tray bakes and sticky maple wings, here are five of our most popular chicken recipes.

item.title: Actor Sam Neill, star of Jurassic Park, dies
item.description: New Zealand actor Sam Neill, who starred in My Brilliant Career and Jurassic Park, has died aged 78.

item.title: Queensland wildlife rescuers prepare for bird flu after sick giant petrel found at Noosa
item.description: A northern giant petrel found at Noosa Main Beach is undergoing testing for H5 avian influenza.

item.title: Actor Sam Neill's final take
item.description: Actor Sam Neill might have officially been a Kiwi but he did some of his best work in Australian film and television. At home on either side of the ditch, this is Sam Neill's story.

item.title: What we know about Christian Jack Joseph, the Perth doctor accused of killing a man in Fremantle
item.description: A young Perth doctor and musician is behind bars, accused of killing a 63-year-old man after a late-night Fremantle scuffle. What do we know?

item.title: Australia has a sperm donor shortage and women are turning to social media and apps
item.description: Australia is facing a sperm donation shortage and it is leading those desperate to find a way to conceive down some expensive and potentially dangerous paths.

item.title: From Jurassic Park to The Piano, here are Sam Neill's best roles
item.description: With more than 150 acting roles under his belt, Sam Neill leaves a lasting legacy to the film and TV industry, and an array of characters that will stay in fans' hearts forever.

item.title: Geelong forward Jeremy Cameron to have AC joint surgery, Hawthorn's Karl Amon has knee procedure
item.description: Geelong are hopeful star forward Jeremy Cameron will return in time to play a part in their premiership push after suffering a brutal shoulder injury.

item.title: Australia facing a sperm donor shortage
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Explain the Internet: Is the controversy over Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey justified?
item.description: The tech trillionaire has spent a good chunk of 2026 yelling on X about how Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's Odyssey "desecrates" the ancient poet.

item.title: Illicit tobacco sales moving from stores to the online marketplace
item.description: The process was simple and the service was speedy but the ease with which 7.30 acquired illicit tobacco products was concerning.

item.title: Spielberg leads outpouring of global tributes for actor Sam Neill
item.description: The New Zealand actor died suddenly at the age of 78, with Hollywood directors and actors and prominent political figures all paying tribute to a great of global cinema.

item.title: Video appears to show Thailand pub fire reached flashover 'point of no return', expert says
item.description: A video analysis of an explosive pub fire in Thailand, which killed 30 people, appears to show a flashover "point of no return" that would have made evacuation nearly impossible, an expert says.

item.title: Wannabe Olympic MMA group tried gifting $82,000 cognac to Brisbane Games committee
item.description: The gifts, revealed exclusively by the ABC today, are part of a wider series of examples of attempted influence-peddling from businesses and sporting bodies before the Games kick off in 2032.

item.title: Four more charged over alleged kidnapping and murder of Chris Baghsarian in Sydney
item.description: A man accused of coordinating the alleged murder and kidnapping of Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian has been charged.

item.title: What happened to Peter Falconio? The outback murder mystery that continues to haunt Australia 25 years on
item.description: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend, Joanne Lees, in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

item.title: Former Victorian Young Australian of the Year charged over imitation guns, police uniforms
item.description: Darcy McGauley-Bartlett has been arrested and charged with a raft of offences, including possessing imitation firearms and police uniforms.

item.title: Ukraine and allies join to protect Europe from ballistic missiles
item.description: Ukraine and nine other countries will form a coalition to protect Europe from ballistic missiles, utilising Kyiv's experience in fighting Russia's full-scale invasion for over four years.

item.title: Teenager charged over alleged stabbing of 15yo boy at Queensland high school
item.description: A teenage boy has been remanded in custody by a court after he was charged over an alleged stabbing at a high school in Far North Queensland. Another boy remains in a stable condition in hospital.

item.title: Will property tax changes stem Australia's productivity crisis?
item.description: With housing prices coming off the boil, maybe our banks will start lending to more productive areas of the economy, like business.

item.title: Dutch referee Rob Dieperink dead at 38 two months after World Cup axing
item.description: Dutch football mourns the sudden death of "highly valued referee" Rob Dieperink just months after FIFA removed him from the panel of World Cup officials.

item.title: Five whale entanglements in five days bring sharks, close beaches on Ningaloo Reef
item.description: Authorities save one animal but another succumbs to its injuries, leading sharks to converge on a popular snorkelling spot.

item.title: Sharks feed on whale carcass off Exmouth
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ICE agents shoot and kill Colombian motorist in Maine
item.description: A federal immigration officer has fatally shot a motorist in Maine, the second time in a week that US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents have used deadly force.

item.title: Former Spanish PM slammed over 'intolerable whiffs of racism' towards France team
item.description: Mariano Rajoy finds himself in hot water after saying France's team "does not have any French players" ahead of the World Cup semifinal.

item.title: Australia's lamb export gains ground in US as protein 'wave' creates demand
item.description: Australians may love their lamb, but it has always been a harder sell for American consumers. There are signs that is changing.

item.title: The four stages of the mental load
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'I lost a normal childhood': What it's like being a young carer
item.description: Kenny and Naomi were both younger than 10 when they became carers for family members.

item.title: Considerable spike in oil price could spell higher petrol prices for motorists
item.description: The recent closure of the Strait of Hormuz saw the oil price lift, but buying in the market accelerated following Donald Trump's latest Truth Social post.

item.title: Bird flu detection training to be rolled out to SA conservation volunteers
item.description: Training to help volunteers identify potential cases of bird flu will be rolled out in South Australia, under a partnership between conservation groups and government.

item.title: Gold Coast Suns extend Jed Walter contract, Western Bulldogs suffer Bailey Dale injury blow
item.description: Shunning big-money offers from rival clubs, Jed Walter said the lure of achieving AFL glory with his childhood club made re-signing a "pretty easy decision".

item.title: Mystery surrounds sudden departure of Mildura Base Public Hospital chief executive
item.description: Mystery surrounds the departure of the chief executive of Mildura Base Public Hospital, who led the north-west Victorian hospital through its transition from private to public.

item.title: Major SA highways given lowest possible safety rating by Australian Road Assessment Program
item.description: Parts of the Sturt Highway, which connects Sydney to Adelaide and provides an integral part of the country's freight network, have been given the lowest possible safety rating by the Australian Road Assessment Program (AusRAP).

item.title: Cyber warning over Russian hackers targeting critical industries
item.description: The Australian Signals Directorate says hackers are trying to access information through network devices, such as laptops, which use old software and predictable passwords.

item.title: Sentencing delayed for Jatin Pudaruth over assault of 87yo Rossmoyne traffic warden
item.description: An 18-year-old assaulted an 87-year-old traffic warden after he subjected the young man's girlfriend to "persistent unwanted attention", a Perth court hears.

item.title: Woman strangled by wheelchair lap belt due to staff shortage, court hears
item.description: A woman who died after slipping from her wheelchair and being strangled by its lap belt had been left unsupervised due to a staff shortage at an Adelaide care facility, a court has heard.

item.title: PBS access to two vital MS treatments under threat
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Launceston General Hospital experiencing 'impacted' capacity due to high demand
item.description: Tasmania's top health official says it will take the Launceston General Hospital "two or three days" to bring its services back to an acceptable level, after it issued a notice urging patients to only attend if it is a medical emergency.

item.title: Anthony Albanese maps out AI future with new national framework
item.description: Labor will develop a new national framework in response to artificial intelligence as it seeks to address the technology’s growing economic and social impacts.

item.title: Buffett severs donations to Gates Foundation following Epstein revelations
item.description: The American investor will end his longtime donations to the Microsoft co-founder's non-profit.

item.title: Art class uses creativity to paint different picture of mental health
item.description: In regional NSW, one program shows that creativity may be the key to better mental health.

item.title: Impressionism's legacy reconsidered in exhibitions at AGSA and Geelong Gallery
item.description: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

item.title: Batteries 'cheaper' than gas plants as data centres fuel turbine costs
item.description: Falling battery costs and a data centre-fuelled dash for gas have overturned decades of accepted wisdom: gas is no longer the cheapest way to meet peak power demand.

item.title: Japan ramps up defences amid concerns about China's 'military activities'
item.description: While the world has been watching the rise of China, Japan has been in the middle of its biggest defence shift since World War II.

item.title: Telstra compensation process puts onus on customers, consumer body says
item.description: Telstra is assessing outage compensation claims, but consumer advocates say the process is cumbersome and puts the onus on customers rather than the telco at fault.

item.title: Australia's best-paid executive lives in US and earns 500 times that of average full-time worker
item.description: Australia's most highly paid executive collected a salary nearly 500 times that of the country's full-time average worker.

item.title: What would Australia's economy look like under One Nation?
item.description: From interest rates to policy advice from billionaire buddy Gina Rinehart, here is how One Nation's economic platform could look.

item.title: Live: US, Iran exchange fire as Trump issues new threats and blockade starts
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he will not charge a 20 per cent toll on traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, as the US military confirms its naval blockade of Iran has come into effect. Follow live.

item.title: Woman sues ex-Catholic Archbishop Julian Porteous over alleged abuse by another priest
item.description: A Tasmanian woman is suing former Archbishop of Hobart Julian Porteous and the state of Tasmania over allegations of sexual abuse by two others when she was a child.

item.title: Fertiliser company Neutrog fined over smelly manure piles at Kanmantoo
item.description: An Adelaide Hills business that piled chicken manure, coffee grounds and other waste up to twice the allowed height has been fined by a South Australian court after more than 700 complaints about the smell.

item.title: New owner of Rushy Lagoon outlines plans for pine, cattle and tourism
item.description: The buyer of Tasmania's largest farm says in addition to the pine plantation it has planned for the property, it's also considering cropping and leasing out parts of the farm for grazing, tourism and mountain biking.

item.title: Sydney hobby clubs tackling loneliness and creating community
item.description: Hobby-based pop-up clubs are attracting people from university students and office workers to new migrants and academics.

item.title: Two people killed in truck crash at Heathcote, north of Melbourne
item.description: A car and truck collided about 9.45pm on the McIvor Highway at Heathcote.

item.title: AI-powered glasses improve everyday life for disability sector amid privacy concerns
item.description: For people who are blind, AI-powered smart glasses deliver a level of independence that was once out of reach, but the rise of wearable cameras also raises urgent questions about surveillance, consent and the law.

item.title: Kakadu's closed waterfalls spark stern warning from NT tourism minister
item.description: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

item.title: Perth man Cameron Hughes's death in Bali leaves trail of debt, customers in limbo
item.description: Cameron Hughes, 39, died in a Balinese immigration detention centre last week under yet-to-be-confirmed circumstances. The ABC can reveal the Perth businessman left behind hundreds of thousands of dollars in unfinished business, with dozens of customers potentially out of pocket.

item.title: Live: Labor to force data centres to minimise water, underwrite own power
item.description: The PM has announced new artificial intelligence data centres will be forced to underwrite or supply their own power and minimise their use of water under new AI standards to be introduced to parliament. Follow live.

item.title: Australia grapples with child psychiatrist shortage as experts warn of consequences
item.description: Experts are warning younger children are presenting with increasingly complex mental health needs as a new report predicts a nationwide shortfall of child and adolescent psychiatrists.

item.title: Live: ASX rises after US reimposes naval blockade on Iran
item.description: The Australian share market has started its day higher after some weaker-than-expected US inflation figures boosted Wall Street and reduced the likelihood of the Federal Reserve lifting interest rates. Follow live.

item.title: Life at Davis Station: 24 minutes of sun after six weeks of darkness
item.description: Researchers spending winter at Australia's southernmost Antarctic research station gathered to celebrate the return of the Sun after weeks of darkness.

item.title: Man shot dead in Sydney's north-west in 'very brazen attack'
item.description: A man has been shot dead in Sydney's north-west, with the homicide squad now leading the investigation.

item.title: Aerial cull prompts push from sporting shooters to target feral deer in WA national parks
item.description: After a second annual aerial cull sparked controversy in southern WA, hunters say targeting feral deer from the ground is a more humane option.

item.title: Writer E Jean Carroll collects $8m in Trump sex abuse and defamation case
item.description: Writer E Jean Carroll has collected nearly $US5.6 million ($8 million) from Donald Trump after a jury in 2023 found the US president liable for sexually abusing and defaming her, court records show.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Spain's secret to success, Lamine Yamal's streak and Folarin Balogun's confession
item.description: Spain showcases its biggest advantage over France, Lamine Yamal's hot streak goes on and Folarin Balogun opens up on his overturned red card. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: World Cup: France falters at first real hurdle against Spain as superstars fail to shine
item.description: France's 2026 World Cup campaign is ended by a poor 2-0 defeat to Spain, as the much-hyped collection of star forwards fail to turn up for the biggest game yet.

item.title: City of Yarra ends e-bike scheme amid claims operator Lime was getting 'free ride'
item.description: Yarra Council says e-bike operator Lime refused to take responsibility for retrieving dumped bikes.

item.title: New political parties are exploiting Victoria's archaic electoral system
item.description: New political parties are seeking to exploit Victoria's anachronistic electoral system in the upper house, to harvest voters' preferences and funnel them wherever they please.

item.title: Scottish traffic cone gets formal Boston welcome after Scots fans' World Cup adventures
item.description: The World Cup's obsession with Scotland fans and traffic cones continues as a witch's hat from Glasgow receives an official welcome from dignitaries in Boston.

item.title: Fair Work Commission upholds sacking of truck driver for $100k ice cream mistake
item.description: The Fair Work Commission rules a trucking company was within its rights to fire a driver after he set the temperature of his trailer full of ice cream more than 20 degrees too warm.

item.title: Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton nicknamed 'Gus' sells for nearly $72m
item.description: One of the largest Tyrannosaurus rex skeletons discovered makes history as the most valuable dinosaur fossil bought at auction.

item.title: Dan Purdie rules out increasing police presence in Queensland schools after stabbings
item.description: Two students were allegedly stabbed in separate instances this week. Two teenagers are due to face court over the alleged attacks today.

item.title: US uses explosive one-way drone boats for first time in combat against Iran
item.description: The United States is using one-way drone boats in combat against Iran for the first time in its military history. A defence expert says America is catching on to this "cheaper and more effective" tactic employed by Iran and Ukraine.

item.title: Kangaroo with joey shot with arrow at Lake Wallace
item.description: Community members search for the injured animal, which was spooked off before wildlife volunteers could help.

item.title: Moira Deeming writes to Victorian Liberal state executive in bid to retain candidacy
item.description: Victorian MP Moira Deeming faces being disendorsed as a Liberal candidate for refusing to apologise to former party leader Matthew Guy, after accusing him of assaulting her at a community event in May.

item.title: Death of man experiencing homelessness in winter prompts call for housing help
item.description: Millicent residents say they are concerned for the welfare of dozens of people sleeping rough in town.

item.title: Have you heard of vaginismus?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Empty-nesters on how they 'fill the void' left by children
item.description: Experts say there can be unexpected grief for parents that comes from children leaving home.

item.title: Townsville gardener witnesses 'garbage guts' tree frog preying on sunbird
item.description: A Townsville gardener was watering her garden when she witnessed a white-lipped tree frog catch a sunbird mid-flight.

item.title: Mollie O'Callaghan reveals spinal stress fractures before Glasgow Commonwealth Games
item.description: Mollie O'Callaghan reveals she has defied doctors' diagnoses to swim at the Commonwealth Games and Pan Pacific Championships despite spinal fractures and a shoulder injury.

item.title: Timor-Leste scam centre compounds uncovered as police arrest Chinese, Indonesians, Cambodians
item.description: Police in Timor-Leste uncover a major international scam centre in the country, arresting more than 300 Chinese, Indonesian and Cambodian nationals in raids across the capital, Dili.

item.title: Melbourne's north-west rocked by two drive-by shootings in one night
item.description: A shooter opened fire on Kealba home, in one of two drive-by shootings in Melbourne's north-west overnight.

item.title: Boy, 5, dies after being hit by 4WD in Cloverdale, in Perth's south-east
item.description: A five-year-old boy dies and a 48-year-old woman is critically injured after being struck by a four-wheel drive outside a park in Cloverdale in Perth's south-east,

item.title: Man denied voluntary assisted dying wins partial victory in early test of ACT law
item.description: A tribunal has ruled on a man's bid to overturn his rejection from the ACT's assisted dying scheme in one of the first tests of how people can challenge such decisions.

item.title: The AFL is set on boutique stadiums for AFLW. Players think it is limiting the game
item.description: The AFL has introduced four double-headers this season, meaning games will be played at bigger venues, while the league remains steadfast on boutique grounds for standalone matches.

item.title: Investigation into Sydney childcare worker expands to South Australian Indigenous community
item.description: A federal police investigation into an alleged childcare abuser has expanded to a remote Indigenous community in South Australia.

item.title: University of Melbourne head decries antisemitism on campus but defends management of it
item.description: The Royal Commission has heard the University of Melbourne has been improving its processes for students to make complaints in the wake of antisemitic experiences by staff and students which escalated in 2024.
